title: Aasa Vachen Lyrics Panchalankurichi
stanzas:
kanagaambaram eduthu
kaiyyaal nee thoduthu
pinnaala vachi vida
aasa vachen

mariyaatha illaama
machaana unna pesi
maaroda mallukatta
aasa vachen

Maaralavu thanniyila
manja thaechi naan
kulikka, maranjirundhu
neeyum paarkka
aasa vachen

passuvap pola
mella vandhu
kosuvathayum nee iluthu
kusumbu panna
venumnnu aasa vachen

kuththaatha mullu kuthi
kudhigaalu valikkudhu
madimela kaala poda
aasa vachen

negaattu moolaiyila
megam karukkayila
sukku thanni
vachi thara
aasa vachen

macham kulirugira
margazhi maasathula
machaana thottu thooonga
aasa vachen

maaman kattum vettiyila
manja kara ennadhunnu
mandhayila ninnu solla
aasa vachen

ritti kolam aasaarikku
pottiyila pana koduthu
retta thottil seiyya
solla aasa vachen

venneera kodhikavechu
machaana kulikka vachi
maaraappu nanaiyathaane
aasa vachen

mandhoppil kattilittu
manam pola thottu thottu
maaman kooda pesidathaan
aasa vachen

seo.title: Aasa Vachen Lyrics Panchalankurichi

enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the enchanting melody of Aasa Vachen from the movie Panchalankurichi, a masterpiece composed by Deva and soulfully rendered by Swarnalatha. This romantic tune captures the essence of love and longing, making it a timeless favorite. Dive into the harmonious blend of heartfelt lyrics and captivating music that continues to resonate with Tamil cinema enthusiasts.
stanzaMeanings: Aasa Vachen Song Lyrics Panchalankurichi #Deva #Swarnalatha
In the orchard, I wished to meet you.
I wished to convey my feelings and show my identity.
I wished to pick the Kanagambaram flower, touch it with my hands, and place it behind.
I wished to speak to you informally and wrestle with you playfully.
Everything has passed, my dear, and now only sadness remains.
I wished to bathe in turmeric water and secretly watch you.
I wished to approach you gently like a cow and tease you playfully.
I wished to buy a saree for you at the village market.
I wished to remove the thorn that pricked me and rest my legs on your lap.
Everything has passed, my dear, and now only sadness remains.
In the orchard, I wished to meet you.
I wished to convey my feelings and show my identity.
I wished to offer you ginger water during the rainy season.
I wished to touch you and sleep beside you during the cold Margazhi month.
I wished to point out the turmeric stain on the cloth tied by my uncle.
I wished to give money to the artist to create a double cradle for the festival.
Everything has passed, my dear, and now only sadness remains.
I wished to tie a red thread around your waist.
I wished to apply good oil to your beautiful body.
I wished to bathe you with warm water and soak the towel.
I wished to place a bed on the terrace and talk to you closely.
Everything has passed, my dear, and now only sadness remains.
In the orchard, I wished to meet you.
I wished to convey my feelings and show my identity.
